devbar.1.18.3: Retry the badge-hide shadow-root injection for quiescent captures (DEV-6497). `suppressFrameworkDevIndicators` dropped its injection when the `nextjs-portal` element was present but its shadow root had not attached yet: the light-DOM MutationObserver sees the portal insertion (shadow still null) but never the later shadow attach — a shadow-DOM mutation it cannot observe — so on a *quiescent* captured page (no further mutations to self-heal) the resting Next.js badge leaked into the screenshot, the exact failure the badge-hide prevents. It now retries for a bounded number of animation frames (`setTimeout` fallback) when the portal is present but its shadow root is not ready, then gives up so a never-attaching portal cannot spin forever.
devbar.1.18.2: Hide the framework's own floating dev indicator (the Next.js dev-tools "N" badge) while it is benign — but never while it is signalling an error. Next renders the badge inside a `nextjs-portal` **shadow root**, fixed to a bottom corner; at rest it duplicates the devbar, sits on top of page content, and pollutes screenshots — and shadow-DOM encapsulation means no capture pipeline can hide it from the outside (a main-document `<style>` or html2canvas `ignoreElements` never reaches into the portal's shadow root). The devbar now injects a hide rule INTO that shadow root on init (`suppressFrameworkDevIndicators`, watching for the portal to (re)mount), so every capture backend — agent-browser, sweetlink CDP/CLI, html2canvas — sees the resting badge hidden. The rule is scoped to `[data-next-badge][data-error="false"]`, so an **errored** badge (`data-error="true"`) stays fully visible: a capture that shows it is telling you the captured page is broken, and hiding that would mask a real defect. `data-error` is live, so the CSS reacts on its own as errors come and go; the `[data-nextjs-dialog-overlay]` error overlay is likewise untouched. Cleaned up on `destroy()`.
devbar.1.18.1: Fix a leaked recording render-interval and a stuck record button (DEV-4874). The `record-start-response` handler now clears the timer the click handler started optimistically before starting its own (the first interval previously leaked and re-rendered forever) and rolls back the optimistic “recording” UI when the daemon reports the start failed. The Sweetlink WS record proxy now forwards `record-start` params (label/viewport) to the daemon instead of dropping them.
devbar.1.18.0: **Security (DEV-4521):** remote `exec-js` over the Sweetlink WebSocket is now OFF by default. The WS has no origin/auth model, so honoring `exec-js` unconditionally was an unauthenticated RCE — any local process (or any page that can reach `ws://localhost:<port>`, which is not gated by same-origin policy) could run arbitrary code in the host page. By default `exec-js` is refused and arbitrary `query-dom` property reflection falls back to the safe summary; the daemon's screenshot/recording/outline/schema/a11y features are unaffected. Opt back in with `initGlobalDevBar({ sweetlink: { allowRemoteExec: true } })` only when you trust everything that can reach the WS port. **Note:** if you rely on `sweetlink exec --js` against the browser, set `allowRemoteExec: true`.
devbar.1.17.0: Hardening + optimization round (DEV-4520). **Bundle:** the browser build (`scripts/build-browser.mjs`) now uses esbuild code-splitting, so axe-core (~570KB) and html2canvas-pro (~200KB) — already `import()`ed lazily in source — are emitted as on-demand chunks instead of inlined. The eager entry drops from ~992KB to ~202KB; the heavy chunks load only when the a11y-audit / screenshot features run. Note: `dist/browser/` now ships entry files + a `chunks/` dir — no-bundler consumers must serve the whole dir. **Security:** viewer URLs arriving over the Sweetlink WebSocket are now scheme-validated (`isSafeNavigationUrl`) before `location.href`/`window.open`, closing a `javascript:`-URL XSS vector. **Leaks:** the recording render-interval, reconnect timeout, and design-review-error timeout are now cleared on disconnect/`destroy()`, and `destroy()` tears down an active ruler overlay. **Annotate (DEV-4516 follow-up):** Escape now resyncs the toolbar control (no stuck-active button); `onMove` skips redundant layout reads via a `lastTarget` short-circuit. **Hygiene:** deduped `clearChildren`, biome-clean. (The unauthenticated `exec-js` RCE over the WebSocket is tracked separately in DEV-4521 — out of scope here.)
devbar.1.16.1: Fix expanded toolbar spacing for inline-only custom controls such as release/version badges. Ungrouped controls no longer enable the grouped plugin responsive layout marker, so a single release badge keeps the toolbar content-sized instead of leaving a wide empty pill. Grouped custom controls still use the grouped responsive layout.
devbar.1.16.0: Live visual-feedback annotation (DEV-4516). New `Annotate` toolbar control: click an element on the running page to pin a region comment (fix/question/praise), which POSTs to the `el-visual-evidence review-ui --listen` server and lands in the same `feedback.json` an active Claude Code session reads on its next capture→fix cycle. Complements the standalone screenshot-review UI — this is the in-context, live-page surface. Configurable via the new `annotate` option (`{ enabled?: boolean; endpoint? }`, default enabled, endpoint `http://localhost:3846/api/feedback`); transport is fire-and-forget `no-cors`+`text/plain` so no CORS/server change is needed. Overlay nodes carry `data-devbar`/`data-devbar-overlay` so visual-evidence capture still hides them. New exports: `activateAnnotateMode`, `registerAnnotateControl`, `buildSelector`, `viewportBucket`, `submitPin`, plus `DevbarPin`/`AnnotateOptions` types. Press Esc (or click the control again) to exit annotate mode.
devbar.1.15.1: Republish of 1.15.0 with the actual `dist/` build of the autoConnect change. 1.15.0 was published from a stale local `dist/` that pre-dated the source edits, so it shipped with the version bump but no runtime/type changes. Use `1.15.1` (or higher); `1.15.0` is deprecated. No source change beyond the rebuild.
devbar.1.15.0: New `sweetlink.autoConnect` option (defaults to `true`). When set to `false`, devbar skips the initial WebSocket connection attempt to the Sweetlink CLI on startup — useful for projects that load devbar but don't run the Sweetlink daemon, where the unsuppressible browser-level `WebSocket connection to 'ws://...' failed: ECONNREFUSED` log on every page load is otherwise pure noise. Apps can still opt in at runtime by calling `globalDevBar.connectWebSocket()`. No behavior change for existing setups (autoConnect defaults to true).
devbar.1.14.7: Small-screen toolbar default: devbar now starts in compact mode at 860px and below while keeping explicit compact preferences and wide desktop expanded mode intact. This prevents mobile/tablet layouts from booting into the denser expanded toolbar.
devbar.1.14.6: Bumps @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.24.3 so workspace:^ resolves to the latest browser bridge reconnect hardening release.
devbar.1.14.5: Plain text tooltips now follow the hovered control color after hover styling is applied, so plugin and custom-control tooltip text matches the toolbar control instead of falling back to the default primary accent.
devbar.1.14.4: Bumps @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.24.1 so workspace:^ resolves to a sweetlink that accepts Vite 8 as a peer.
devbar.1.14.3: Simulated-mobile toolbar density pass: action icons now use compact 32px controls instead of touch-target sizing, and inline plugin badges such as release metadata drop the extra mobile padding/min-height that created oversized whitespace.
devbar.1.14.2: Responsive layout fix for staging release badges: md and smaller toolbars now stack custom controls, status, and actions as content-sized rows instead of stretching each row to the full available toolbar width.
devbar.1.14.1: Bugfix for staging release badges: async Sweetlink settings now reinject theme CSS when a loaded setting changes light/dark mode, so the settings UI and visible toolbar no longer drift. Ungrouped plugin controls such as `releaseInfoPlugin()` now render inline with the main toolbar instead of forcing a wide, mostly empty second row.
devbar.1.14.0: Release metadata plugin and custom-control hover tooltips. New `releaseInfoPlugin()` registers a compact badge with release timestamp, optional version, and changelog hover text for staging/preview deploys. `DevBarControl` now accepts a `tooltip` string or callback so host apps can add hover detail to any custom control without writing rendering code.
devbar.1.13.0: **Hardening release** — security fixes, type-design tightening, and code dedup from a comprehensive product-finalizer audit. **Type design (potentially breaking for type users):** `LogSubscribeCommand.filters.levels` and `LogSubscription.filters.levels` now use the canonical `ConsoleLogLevel` union instead of an inline drift-prone literal — `trace`-level subscriptions now compile. New `AccentColor` alias documents the intent on `accentColor` fields. `DevBarState.consoleFilter` is now `Extract<ConsoleLogLevel, 'error' | 'warn' | 'info'> | null` so the link to the canonical level union is explicit. New `AxeImpact` literal alias used by `getViolationCounts`/`groupViolationsByImpact`/`getImpactColor` (with runtime fallback for back-compat). New `OutlineCategory` literal union replaces `category?: string`. `MicrodataItem` is now exported and `MetaImage`/`MissingTag`/`OutlineCategory` are re-exported through the package index. **Cleanup:** deleted unused `formatBytes`/`formatDuration`/`getInitiatorColor` from network.ts, `getBadgeColor`/`clearA11yCache`/`getCachedResult`/`formatViolation` from accessibility.ts, `clearLocalStorage`/`clearSessionStorage`/`formatStorageSummary`/`beautifyJson` from storage.ts, and the `earlyConsoleCapture.ts`/`outline.ts`/`schema.ts` shim files (consumers re-export direct from sweetlink via the same package-index name). 5× `'#ef4444'` literals in buttons.ts and rgba literals in collapsed.ts now use `PALETTE.red`/`withAlpha(PALETTE.red, 95)` token-based forms. `DevBarControl` shape is now imported from a single source instead of duplicated inline in 3 rendering modules; `TOOLBAR_POSITIONS` and `TAILWIND_BREAKPOINT_ROWS` extracted from rendering helpers. Bumps @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.24.0.
devbar.1.12.1: Expanded toolbar text color now follows the accent color, matching the border. Picking a non-emerald accent (or carrying a custom hex from `initGlobalDevBar`) no longer produces the mismatched red-border/green-text look. Collapsed and compact layouts already followed accent; this aligns expanded.
devbar.1.12.0: Browser-bundle entry for no-bundler consumers. New `@ytspar/devbar/browser` and `@ytspar/devbar/browser/auto` exports ship pre-bundled, self-contained ESM (`dist/browser/devbar.js` and `dist/browser/devbar.auto.js`) that resolve all transitive bare imports — `@ytspar/sweetlink/...`, `axe-core`, `html2canvas-pro` — into a single file. Apps without a frontend bundler (vanilla Node http servers, static hosts, server-rendered HTML strings) can now serve the file directly from `node_modules` without adding esbuild/rollup themselves: `<script type="module" src="/path/to/node_modules/@ytspar/devbar/dist/browser/devbar.auto.js"></script>` auto-initializes on DOMContentLoaded; the non-auto entry exports `initGlobalDevBar` for manual config. Build now runs an internal esbuild step (added as a devDependency); existing Vite/Next.js consumers using `@ytspar/devbar` continue to work unchanged. Settings popover accent picker now correctly highlights Emerald when the persisted accent is the theme-default CSS variable (previously no swatch showed as active for fresh installs), and shows a small `custom · reset` pill when the accent is a non-preset value so users with a custom hex (e.g. `#ef4444`) can return to the theme default without clearing localStorage.
devbar.1.11.1: Theme defaults and host-enforced theme modes. DevBar options now support `defaultThemeMode` for first-run/reset defaults and `themeMode` for apps that need to force dark/light/system, so dark-only host apps do not inherit a light OS preference from `system`. Settings copy now accurately describes `system` as OS/browser preference. Canary and production release workflows now publish with `pnpm publish`, no longer self-upgrade npm on the runner, use Node 24 action runtimes, and pin pnpm to the repo's 9.15.9 toolchain.
devbar.1.11.0: Portless compatibility for proxied local development. devbar now reads Sweetlink runtime connection hints instead of assuming the visible browser port is the internal app port, supports manual `sweetlink` overrides (`appPort`, `wsPort`, `wsUrl`, `wsPath`), tries same-origin WebSocket endpoints before direct localhost ports, and keeps explicit-port localhost workflows working. Bumps @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.23.0.
devbar.1.10.8: Responsive toolbar and docs-site hardening. Custom/plugin controls now keep long Web Vitals, action buttons, and plugin rows inside the expanded toolbar at desktop and tablet widths; phone expanded toolbar spacing is tighter while preserving 44px touch targets. The playground homepage adds a plugin-surface section with an interactive Zaraz consent-debug, git branch, and app-version demo plus a concrete consentDebugPlugin example. Mobile package cards and fixed simulated-mode controls are guarded by new screenshot/evidence e2e coverage. Bumps @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.22.1 for automatic Playwright screenshot hiding helpers.
devbar.1.10.7: Bump @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.22.0. Modal accessibility: dialog elements now carry `aria-label` reflecting their title and auto-focus on open (Outline, Schema, A11y, Console, Design Review, Consent Debug). Code cleanup: deleted dead `ui/cards.ts` (163 lines), collapsed `modules/rendering.ts` barrel, pruned unused html2canvas helpers, extracted `addSavedTooltip` for the 4 button creators. Test fix: axe e2e scope corrected (`include` selector moved from `RunOptions` to context arg — was scanning entire page). Responsive plugin hardening: custom toolbar controls now wrap inside expanded and compact devbar layouts, cap long labels, center mobile group labels, preserve 44px touch targets, and ship e2e screenshots for arbitrary plugin controls at 320, 390, 640, 836, and 993px.
devbar.1.10.5: Bump @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.20.0 (multi-capture JSON-stdin batching + app/date/run directory hierarchy).
devbar.1.10.4: Bump @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.19.0 (terminal capture mode + iOS/Android simulator screen recording — `proof`-style evidence-of-work for non-browser tests).
devbar.1.10.3: Bump @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.18.0 (recording pause/resume, sessions list+index, record-exec DSL, --label/--theme/--storage-state/--trace flags, click-css occlusion detection, network body capture, visual-diff side-by-side viewer, brighter cursor highlight, real action durations, ISO session names, failure screenshots, compact CLI output — 20 UX wins).
devbar.1.10.2: Bump @ytspar/sweetlink to 1.17.0 (recording bug fixes, screenshot full-page dim reporting, snapshot annotation full-page capture, click-css daemon action, action-screenshot click marker, ISO timestamps, and 14 more UX polishes — see sweetlink 1.17.0 release notes for details).
devbar.1.10.1: Republish of 1.10.0 via pnpm so the @ytspar/sweetlink dependency resolves to a real version instead of `workspace:^`. 1.10.0 is deprecated — use 1.10.1.
devbar.1.10.0: Consent-debug plugin (@ytspar/devbar/plugins/consent-debug): toolbar control gated on window.zaraz that opens a modal for mocking geo regions, triggering banner/notice events, Accept/Reject/Clear/Reset actions, and a live status readout — generic cookie and event names
devbar.1.9.2: Demo button + HiFi screenshot (Alt+Click) in toolbar, recording auto-opens viewer, demo document state fields
devbar.1.9.1: Bump sweetlink dependency to v1.14.0 (persistent daemon, ref system, ring buffers, session recording)
devbar.1.9.0: Official plugins (git-branch, app-version), server info on state (gitBranch, appName), PALETTE.white, root tsconfig with project references, 232 new tests
devbar.1.8.4: Fix custom controls not showing: store controls on window to survive bundler module duplication, render controls in compact mode
devbar.1.8.0: Shadow theme tokens, CSS_COLORS theming fixes, function extraction, noUncheckedIndexedAccess
devbar.1.7.5: Correct ruler icon SVG, PALETTE color system centralization
devbar.1.7.1: Ruler tool for measuring element dimensions on-page
devbar.1.6.4: Console log sync, CLI shutdown coordination
devbar.1.6.0: Inline DepartureMono font, capture browser-level errors
devbar.1.5.3: CSP bypass via Playwright fallback for exec/click/query
devbar.1.5.0: Control groups, info variant, and non-interactive badges
devbar.1.4.0: DRY modal copy/download, pre-release audit, expanded tests
devbar.1.3.0: Accessibility audit, document outline, schema extraction
devbar.1.2.0: Screenshot quality slider, settings crash fix
devbar.1.0.0: Initial release — breakpoints, vitals, console, screenshots

sweetlink.1.27.0: Make automated capture fast enough to sit in a pre-commit gate. Two fixes plus a new batch mode. **Daemon reuse:** the persistent daemon was gated behind `--hifi`/`--responsive`, so automated callers (visual-evidence gallery frames, batch sweeps) fell through to a fresh browser launch per frame — and could not opt in, because the isolated component render routes they capture have no devbar, so there is never a connected browser client and the WebSocket path always escalates. Escalation now prefers the daemon, falling back to a standalone launch only when the daemon cannot serve the request (`--hover` still goes straight to standalone). The standalone path also returned a uniform-gray blank frame for a variant the daemon renders correctly, so this is a correctness fix as much as a speed one — blank frames are what make a vision-review gate impassable rather than merely slow (DEV-6729, DEV-6692). **`--batch <manifest.json|->`:** captures a whole frame list in ONE process — one server wait, one daemon handshake, N frames — because once the browser launch was gone, Node startup per invocation dominated. Measured on 10 full-page gallery frames with the daemon already warm: 3318ms/frame via a shell loop, 101ms/frame via `--batch` (32.7x); a 26-frame plan drops from ~86s to ~2.6s. Output is byte-identical to the sequential path. A failed frame is reported and the batch continues. **Portless URLs:** `http://localhost:3000` stopped being a safe default once apps moved behind Portless, which assigns the underlying port dynamically and exports the real route as `PORTLESS_URL` — `sweetlink status` reported on a dead port and every URL-less command aimed at the wrong app. `defaultDevUrl()` now resolves `SWEETLINK_DEV_URL` → `PORTLESS_URL` → `http://localhost:3000`, leaving non-Portless projects unaffected.
sweetlink.1.26.4: Keep wide screenshots/video inside their containers in the two self-contained HTML review UIs. In the session viewer (`viewer.ts`), the video/screenshot is a flex item whose default `min-width: auto` could refuse to shrink below the media's intrinsic width despite `max-width: 100%` — a wider-than-pane capture then overflowed the `.video-container`; added `min-width: 0; min-height: 0` so it clamps. In the visual-diff viewer (`visualDiff.ts`): `.stack` (overlay view) gets `overflow: hidden` so the absolutely-positioned `current` overlay image can no longer bleed past the baseline-sized figure box; plain `img` gets `height: auto` to preserve aspect ratio when clamped; and side-by-side `figure` min-width becomes `min(300px, 100%)` so narrow viewports no longer force horizontal page scroll.
sweetlink.1.26.3: Fix the agent-bridge WebSocket resolution under HTTPS proxies, plus daemon navigation/lifecycle hardening. A proxied origin (e.g. an app behind an HTTPS dev proxy) derived the WS port from `location.port` arithmetic, landing on a wrong — and sometimes Chrome-restricted (`ERR_UNSAFE_PORT`) — port. The `NEXT_PUBLIC_SWEETLINK_*` hints injected by `withSweetlink()` are now read as literal member expressions so webpack/Turbopack inline them into the client bundle, and a discovery fallback keeps client and server agreeing on the real endpoint so port math can never land on a blocked port.
sweetlink.1.26.2: Hide the devbar with `opacity: 0`, not just `visibility: hidden`, so an EXPANDED devbar can't leak into screenshots. The screenshot hide injected `visibility: hidden` on `[data-devbar]`; when the devbar is expanded, its toolbar buttons/icons are descendants that set their own `visibility`, escaping the container-level rule so the bar rendered in the shot (observed in an el-visual-evidence control-panel capture: the full icon row + Annotate button appeared despite the hide). `opacity: 0` compounds down the subtree — a descendant cannot render above an ancestor's `opacity: 0` — so it hides the whole bar regardless of descendant styles; explicit descendant (` *`) selectors and `[data-devbar-ruler]` are added as belt-and-suspenders. Also hides two third-party dev overlays that pollute screenshots — TanStack Query Devtools (`[class*="tsqd-"]`) and the Next.js dev-tools indicator — scoped to the specific launcher, NOT the whole `nextjs-portal` (which also hosts the error overlay you want captured).
sweetlink.1.26.1: Fix the bundled `resize-for-claude` Claude skill (DEV-5303). The skill referenced a `scripts/resize-for-claude` script that no longer exists anywhere (the consuming tools repo deleted it as 'duplicated the skill' while the skill only documented it), and its `TOOLS_ROOT` derivation resolved to the *current project's* root rather than the tools repo. The 124-line `sips` resize script (recovered from tools history) is now bundled inside the skill directory and invoked via the skill's own base directory — no external-repo lookup, works from any repo the skill is linked into. Also: `bash`-prefix invocation documented (npm packing drops the executable bit), stale `-partN` tiles cleared on re-run, corrected tall-image example to real output, and a friendly error on non-image input instead of raw `sips` stderr.
sweetlink.1.26.0: Public devbar-hide constants (DEV-4982). The canonical screenshot-hide rule — `HIDE_DEVBAR_CSS` and `HIDE_DEVBAR_STYLE_ID`, plus `SELECTOR_TIMEOUT_MS` / `HOVER_TRANSITION_DELAY_MS` — is now importable from the main `@ytspar/sweetlink` entry and from the dedicated `@ytspar/sweetlink/screenshotConstants` subpath. Previously these lived in an unexported internal module, so downstream consumers (the el-visual-evidence CLI in vertical-int/tools) had to hand-maintain a copy of the `[data-devbar*]` hide CSS that drifted from the source. No runtime behavior change — pure export-surface addition.
sweetlink.1.25.0: Motion-evidence enablement + recorder hardening (DEV-4874). New daemon-free exports `@ytspar/sweetlink/daemon/viewer` (incl. `buildViewerFromDir`), `/daemon/session`, and `/daemon/summary`, so external tools (e.g. el-visual-evidence) can build the interactive session viewer from a WebM + manifest on disk without running the daemon. `SessionManifest` now carries `viewport` — the viewer scales click-ripple overlays to the real recording size instead of a hardcoded 1280×720 — plus `droppedWhilePaused`; `RecordedAction` gains a `step` member for externally-driven flows. Recorder hardening: pause-aware live duration, a surfaced warning when a stop finalizes with no video, a 25MB inline-video cap to avoid OOM/un-openable viewers, and a stopping-guard that stops in-flight action logging from racing teardown.
sweetlink.1.24.6: Restore `claude-skills/resize-for-claude/SKILL.md` (DEV-4814). `@ytspar/sweetlink@1.24.5` silently dropped the file from the published tarball — the `evals/` directory remained but the 97-line `SKILL.md` was missing. The skill is referenced by `tools/claude/skills/figma-design/SKILL.md` as the canonical 'prepare image for Claude vision' path (resize to ≤1568px longest side, split very tall ≥3:1 images into overlapping tiles); the tools-side symlink had to be removed in DEV-4697 Phase 2 because the broken target was breaking catalog generation. No source/runtime change — the file already exists on `main` (commit 8582aff) and the `files` array already covers `claude-skills/`; this release just bumps the version so the publish pipeline ships the now-complete directory. Downstream consumers (e.g. tools) can re-add the `claude/skills/resize-for-claude → @ytspar/sweetlink/claude-skills/resize-for-claude` symlink once they upgrade to 1.24.6.
sweetlink.1.24.5: Claude-skill prose dedupe (DEV-4697). The three Sweetlink-driven skills (`screenshot`, `console-check-sweetlink`, `responsive-screenshots`) re-described Sweetlink architecture, prerequisites, the full CLI surface, and daemon lifecycle inline — each skill carried its own copy that drifted from `claude-context/sweetlink-architecture.md`. Each skill now opens with a one-line pointer to the canonical context and keeps only its own orchestration scope: tool-choice decision flow + output-path policy + per-skill call patterns (screenshot), zero-error policy + error-pattern table + composition (console-check-sweetlink), breakpoint matrix + per-viewport quality checklist + responsive-failure cheat sheet (responsive-screenshots). Trim totals — screenshot 506→143, console-check-sweetlink 330→110, responsive-screenshots 366→123 (1202→376 lines, ~69% reduction). No behavior change; sweetlink CLI surface and bridge runtime unchanged. Downstream consumers (e.g. the tools repo) pick up the trimmed skills automatically when they re-resolve the `@ytspar/sweetlink` dependency — their `claude/skills/<name>` entries symlink into the pnpm store, so no manual skill re-sync is needed. Skills now defer live flag details to `pnpm sweetlink <cmd> --help` rather than the canonical context, which lags newer command flags (DEV-4697 follow-up; canonical-doc refresh tracked separately).
sweetlink.1.24.4: UI Verification Mandate: add a 'Coverage — scale verification to the change' section. Visual evidence must show the change in context in the running app (component-library/gallery views are secondary), scaled to the change: in-context page capture, per-state frames for interactive elements, all-path frames for multi-step/branching elements, and a process storyboard for complex multi-stage features. Reuse-before-create (expand an existing e2e spec instead of duplicating) and durable, CI-runnable specs committed to the e2e suite.
sweetlink.1.24.3: Browser bridge reconnect hardening republish. Expected local WebSocket connection failures are debug-only instead of console.error spam, and pending reconnect or port-scan timers are cancelled on destroy so unmounted bridges cannot open new sockets later.
sweetlink.1.24.2: Browser bridge reconnect hardening. Expected local WebSocket connection failures are now debug-only instead of console.error spam, and pending reconnect/port-scan timers are cancelled on destroy so unmounted bridges cannot open new sockets later.
sweetlink.1.24.1: Widens the `vite` peer-dependency range to include `^8.0.0` (was `^5 || ^6 || ^7`). Vite 8 is GA (8.0.10 latest) and consumers on Vite 8 were hitting peer-conflict errors on install. Sweetlink does not import from vite at runtime — the peer is for the optional vite plugin / dev integration only — so the broader range is safe.
sweetlink.1.24.0: **Security & correctness hardening release** — five critical security fixes, ten races/leaks, eight edge cases, and twenty-plus type-design tightenings + dedups from a multi-agent product-finalizer audit. **Path-traversal cluster (P0):** browser-supplied slugs (HMR `trigger` field, daemon `generate-viewer` `sessionDir`, `visual-diff` `outputPath`) are now sanitised + confined to projectRoot. **WS recording proxy origin-pin (P0):** `record-start`/`record-stop`/`hifi-screenshot` now refuse WS clients whose origin doesn't match the daemon's configured app port — a malicious page on a different localhost port can no longer trigger them. **Viewer XSS (P0):** new `jsonForScript()` `<` → `\u003c` encoder applied to all 5 `JSON.stringify` interpolations inside the viewer's `<script>` block — captured console messages containing `</script>` can no longer break out. **Race/leak fixes (P1):** CLI request routing replaced `cliClientMap` (single-slot) with `cliClientQueues` FIFO so concurrent CLI clients no longer clobber each other's responses. `pendingRequests` switched from `Map<url,_>` to `WeakMap<Request,_>` so parallel same-URL requests no longer collide and GC reclaims entries. Recording rolls back state when `goto()` throws (no permanent lockout). `safeRegex` now blocks alternation-with-quantifier patterns like `(a|aa)+`. Daemon writes its state file inside the listen() callback before resolving so external clients never observe "port open, state missing". `acquireLock` stores `${pid}:${token}` and cross-references the daemon state file to defeat PID reuse; recovery is atomic via wx-write + read-back token verification. `checkRefStale`→action TOCTOU defeated by capturing page URL at handler entry and aborting if it changes before the action. `computeLCS` capped at 4M cells to prevent quadratic DoS on pathologically large snapshots. Recording rename failure falls back to copy+unlink so cross-device EXDEV no longer orphans the video. **Type design:** `DaemonResponse` is now a discriminated union `{ok:true, data?, error?:never} | {ok:false, error, data?}`. `DaemonRequest` keyed by action via `DaemonActionParams` map — each variant carries the params shape its action accepts. `Save*Command` collapsed to `SaveCommand<T,P>` with payload type narrowing; `*ErrorCommand` collapsed to `ErrorCommand<T>`. `daemonRequest(action: DaemonAction)`. New `ScreenshotOptions`/`RefreshOptions` interfaces replace `Record<string,unknown>` escape hatches. `ViewportName = 'mobile'|'tablet'|'desktop'|\`${number}x${number}\`` template-literal union. `MicrodataItem` exported; `MetaImage`/`MissingTag`/`OutlineCategory` re-exported. **CLI refactor:** the 4244-line CLI's 28-case switch is now a dispatch over named handlers (`handleScreenshotCmd`, `handleRecordCmd`, etc.) — each command is independently testable. **Dependencies:** vite override → 7.3.2 patches the dev path-traversal advisory (12 → 3 dev-only audit advisories; production audit reports 0 vulns). **Tests:** 149 new unit tests across 17 modules — daemon listeners (24), recording state machine (21), session manifest types (5), viewer XSS encoder (4), browser pre-init guards (5), urlUtils slug sanitization (3), e2e harness pure helpers (9), devbar types (13), font-data smoke (4), demoMode (9), evidenceContext (7), ruler activation (6), commands/logs filter (6), daemon/cursor (4), daemon/utils (10), daemon/evidence (8), screenshotConstants (4), simulator/env (5).
sweetlink.1.23.0: Portless and named-localhost routing support. Vite integration now exposes a same-origin `/__sweetlink` WebSocket endpoint and injects runtime app/WebSocket port hints so HTTPS Portless URLs with hidden internal ports connect automatically. Next.js integration injects public Sweetlink port hints. Browser bridge/devbar shared helpers now support explicit wsUrl/wsPath/appPort/wsPort overrides. Server origin handling accepts `.localhost`, `.test`, and LAN `.local` development origins, avoids false internal-port warnings for named proxy origins, and keeps direct localhost port mismatch warnings.
sweetlink.1.22.1: Playwright screenshot auto-hide helpers. `installAutoHideDevbarScreenshots(page)` patches `page.screenshot()` so every Playwright user screenshot temporarily hides `[data-devbar]`, overlays, and tooltips, then restores them after capture. `installAutoHideDevbarScreenshotsForContext(context)` applies the same behavior to existing and future pages in a BrowserContext. Existing explicit paths remain: CLI `--hide-devbar`, `screenshotViaPlaywright({ hideDevbar: true })`, `withHiddenDevbarForScreenshot(page, ...)`, and browser `prepareForCapture({ hideDevbar: true })`. Docs now include a Playwright fixture example.
sweetlink.1.22.0: **Hardening release** — 12 security/bug fixes + 11 type-design improvements + 10 cleanups from a multi-agent pre-release audit. **Security (P0):** XSS in term player .html — recorded shell output containing `</script>` previously broke out of the inline data block; now escaped via `escapeJsonForScript` (also handles `<!--`, U+2028/U+2029). Path traversal — `--app ../../tmp/x` and `--run ../../etc/cron.d` no longer escape `.sweetlink/` (segments are slugified). Batch JSON `--output` now refuses paths outside the project root by default (`SWEETLINK_ALLOW_OUTSIDE_OUTPUT=1` opts out). **Bug class (P1):** spawned commands now have `error` handlers + try/finally cleanup so missing-shell/bad-cwd no longer orphan recorders or `getevent` listeners. `term`/`sim` default to a minimal env (PATH/HOME/USER/LANG/SHELL/etc.) so secrets in env (NPM_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, …) can't leak into recorded artifacts; `--inherit-env` opts back in. `visualDiff` distinguishes baseline-missing (ENOENT on baseline) from real IO errors and surfaces the message instead of returning `100% mismatch`. `term player` parses asciicast safely (try/catch with diagnostic, version validation, refuses empty events, clamps duration). `getevent -l` parsing rejects non-hex/out-of-range values and clamps to screen bounds (was producing NaN that silently broke ffmpeg). Android pull now polls for stat-size stability before `adb pull` (replacing a fixed 500ms sleep that caused truncated mp4s on slow devices). Tap-capture proc waits for `close` and flushes its trailing buffer (taps in the last few hundred ms before SIGTERM are no longer dropped). **Type design:** dropped 8 stale `demo-*` entries from `DaemonAction` that had no handlers; tightened `ConsoleLog.level` from `string`-erased to `ConsoleLogLevel` literal union; new shared `term/cast.ts` types (recorder + player); new `RecordedAction` union for `logAction`; dropped `''` empty-string sentinel from `RunSlotOptions.kind`. **Cleanup:** deleted unused `term/ansi.ts` (202 lines); consolidated 3 copies of `escapeHtml` and 2 of `detectGit` into `daemon/utils.ts`; new `findLatestSessionDir`, `openInBrowser`, `resolveCapturePath` helpers (batch path no longer bypasses `runSlot()`); replaced nested ternaries with `thresholdColor` helper for Web Vitals. **Deps:** pnpm overrides for picomatch/postcss/brace-expansion (18 → 6 audit advisories; remaining 6 are dev-only — vite 7 dev server + basic-ftp transitive via puppeteer-core proxy chain). 14 new unit tests (cast.test.ts, runs.test.ts).
sweetlink.1.21.0: **Android tap-indicator overlays** for `sim android` recordings. While `adb shell screenrecord` captures the screen, a parallel `adb shell getevent -l` listener parses BTN_TOUCH DOWN/UP + ABS_MT_POSITION_X/Y events into (x, y, t) tuples. Coordinates are scaled from raw input-device units to screen pixels using `wm size`. After the test command exits, `ffmpeg drawbox` composites a red hollow ring + centred solid dot at each tap position for 0.6s, baked into the .mp4. A `.taps.json` sidecar is always written; if ffmpeg isn't on PATH the raw recording is preserved and a clear hint is logged. `--no-overlays` opts out entirely. 14 unit tests (parser + filter builder) + 2 e2e tests synthesise a black mp4 and verify red rings render at the right times — visually confirmed at the (120, 200) coordinate. iOS tap overlays remain a follow-up requiring `idb_companion`.
sweetlink.1.20.0: Two more `proof`-parity items: **multi-capture JSON-stdin batching** and **app/date/run directory hierarchy**. `cat captures.json | sweetlink --json` reads `{action:'capture',captures:[...]}` and runs each entry (term/sim-ios/sim-android/screenshot), aggregating results into a single envelope on stdout — failing entries set `ok:false` for the run, exit code 1. `--app <name>` groups artifacts under `.sweetlink/<app>/<YYYYMMDD>/<run>/<kind>/...`; `--run <id>` (or `SWEETLINK_RUN` env) pins the run id for related captures; without `--app` the historical flat layout is preserved. `--app` no longer triggers `discoverServer` for term/sim (they don't need a WS bridge), so a typo'd app name doesn't error out artifact-producing commands. 9 new e2e tests in `e2e/batch-mode.spec.ts` (5) and `e2e/run-hierarchy.spec.ts` (4). Curl-pipe-sh installer was considered and skipped — sweetlink is npm-distributed, the installer would be a thin wrapper around `npm i -g`.
sweetlink.1.19.0: Two new capture modes inspired by automazeio/proof — turning sweetlink into evidence-of-work for non-browser tests. **`sweetlink term <command>`** runs any shell command (pytest, go test, make build) and writes asciicast v2 (`.cast`) plus a self-contained HTML player with play/pause, 0.1×–4× speed control, seek bar, and ANSI colour rendering. Auto-speed up for long recordings, exit-code propagation by default (CI fails when wrapped tests fail; `--ignore-exit` opts out). **`sweetlink sim ios|android <command>`** records the iOS Simulator (via `xcrun simctl io recordVideo`) or Android Emulator (via `adb shell screenrecord`) screen while the test command runs, producing an h264 .mp4 of what was on screen. iOS path tested end-to-end on iPhone 16e (95KB / 2.5s native-res capture verified). Player UX confirmed visually: ANSI colours render, speed controls work, seek bar follows playback. New e2e specs: `e2e/term-mode.spec.ts` (5 tests) and `e2e/sim-mode.spec.ts` (6 tests with platform-aware skips).
sweetlink.1.18.0: Round 2 of UX polish — 20 improvements ranging from output cleanup to new features. Quick wins: compact `screenshot ✓ path · WxH · KB · method` one-liner; ActionEntry.duration now populated from actual wall-clock; session IDs are ISO timestamps (`session-2026-04-26T19-47-14`) instead of millisecond epochs; cross-platform viewer auto-open via `open`/`xdg-open`/`cmd /c start`; daemon prints an idle warning at 80% of timeout; top-level `--help` lists one-line summaries with `--all` for full per-command details; --json now works for status/cleanup/wait. Recording: action screenshots stash to `.sweetlink/failures/` on click/fill errors with diagnostic name; `record start --label "login flow"` embeds in manifest + SUMMARY title; `record pause` / `record resume` gap the action timeline so idle windows don't pollute the recording; cursor click ripple now larger, longer, with persistent center dot. Sessions: `sweetlink sessions list` shows every recording with label/duration/error counts; `sessions open` opens an auto-generated `.sweetlink/index.html` browse page; `sessions diff <a> <b>` compares two recordings (action sequences, durations, error counts). Big features: `record exec "click @e2; fill @e3 hello; press Enter"` runs a one-liner DSL with auto start/stop; `record start --storage-state path.json` for testing logged-in flows; `record start --trace` writes Playwright `trace.zip` for `playwright show-trace`; `screenshot --theme dark` emulates `prefers-color-scheme`; `network-read --withBody` captures truncated request/response bodies; `visual-diff` writes a side-by-side `.diff.html` with toggle/overlay (CSS difference blend mode) — far more actionable than just a percentage; `click-css` occlusion check detects overlay covering and reports `covered by <#modal>` instead of a 30s timeout. 10 new e2e tests in `e2e/wave-features.spec.ts`.
sweetlink.1.17.0: TDD audit shipping 9 daemon/CLI bug fixes + 17 UX polishes. Fixes: recording manifest now counts in-session console errors and network failures (was hardcoded 0); screenshots and CSS-selector clicks during recording route to the recording page and get logged as actions; --full-page reports actual PNG dims (not viewport); click-ref/fill-ref fail fast on disabled/non-fillable elements (was 30s hang); console-read/network-read auto-init the browser so first calls aren't empty; daemon status/stop honour --url. New: click-css daemon action, --padding flag for selector screenshots, multi-match selector warning, --full-page hint when content overflows, --responsive defaults to fullPage, viewer.html references session.webm instead of inlining (~280× smaller HTML), recording action screenshots show a red click marker, ruler dim labels positioned outside bbox with bigger font, ruler captures full page when elements extend past viewport, snapshot --annotate captures full page + filters off-screen refs, SUMMARY action timeline is humanized and links to action screenshots, SUMMARY uses ISO 8601 timestamps with timezone, SUMMARY surfaces git-not-in-repo state, recording viewport now matches DEFAULT_VIEWPORT (1512×982). 52 new e2e tests gating these in `e2e/*-bugs.spec.ts`.
sweetlink.1.16.1: Add `default` condition to subpath exports so non-ESM consumers (Node --test, tsx in CJS) can resolve imports like @ytspar/sweetlink/browser/consoleCapture without hitting ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED
sweetlink.1.16.0: Bundle Claude Code skills (screenshot, console-check, responsive-screenshots, resize-for-claude), agent (sweetlink-visual-iterative), and context files (architecture, CDP guide, UI verification mandate) — shipped with npm package for automatic discovery
sweetlink.1.15.0: Demo document builder (Showboat-inspired), universal sharing (report --clipboard/--serve/--webhook), viewer share buttons (Copy Report, Download), multi-language server error detection, SUMMARY.md auto-generation, git metadata in recordings, overlay toggle, responsive viewer, HiFi screenshots from devbar (Alt+Click), comparison tables in docs
sweetlink.1.14.0: Sweetlink v2: persistent Playwright daemon (--hifi, --responsive), @ref system from accessibility tree (snapshot -i, click @e3, fill @e4), snapshot diffing (-D) and annotated screenshots (-a), always-on console/network ring buffers, session recording with interactive viewer, cursor highlights, visual diff, device emulation, PR evidence upload
sweetlink.1.13.0: Next.js plugin: withSweetlink() config wrapper for zero-config dev server integration, auto-detects port from --port argv
sweetlink.1.12.1: Fix navigateBrowser URL verification: poll until actual URL matches target to prevent premature capture on SPAs
sweetlink.1.12.0: App identity: server detects git branch/app name, CLI --app flag targets specific instance for parallel worktree dev
sweetlink.1.11.1: Biome formatting and lint, README updates
sweetlink.1.11.0: --url browser navigation, optional peerDeps for puppeteer-core/html2canvas-pro, docs overhaul
sweetlink.1.10.0: --json and --output-schema flags for structured CLI output
sweetlink.1.9.0: Wait-for selector, async exec, return statement auto-wrap
sweetlink.1.8.1: Console log buffer sync, 209 new tests
sweetlink.1.8.0: CLI shutdown synchronization with dev server lifecycle
sweetlink.1.6.0: Console log eviction sync, browser error capture
sweetlink.1.4.0: Dead code removal, deduplicated constants, 85 new tests
sweetlink.1.3.0: CDP and ruler test suites, ruler/playwright sub-exports
sweetlink.1.2.0: Outline and schema tools moved from devbar, modal UX
sweetlink.1.1.0: Auto-detect save location, browser download mode
sweetlink.1.0.0: Initial release — screenshots, logs, HMR capture, CLI
